type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby functions only, ~lines 1160-1230)
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py (any other functions, e.g. do_sort or elsewhere)
note: My task only touches the groupby filter functions to add a case_sensitive param. If your feature touches a different function in filters.py we can both edit the same file without conflict as long as we stick to our own functions. Let me know what your feature needs to change.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby - case_sensitive param)
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby - with_counts param)
note: Our features target the exact same two functions, so a clean disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequence our edits instead. I'll implement case_sensitive first since I add sorting-key logic that changes the internals (uses ignore_case postprocessor + preserves original-case key), then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE, then re-read the file fresh and layer your with_counts param on top so we don't clobber each other. I'll start now - ok?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Our features both edit the exact same two functions (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby) so a disjoint split isn't possible - we need to serialize instead of parallelize. Proposal: I'll go first since my change (with_counts) is a small additive change (new bool param, count during iteration, conditional yield of 2-tuple vs 3-tuple). I'll save a copy of the pre-edit file, make my edit, then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE before you start editing filters.py, then apply your case_sensitive change on top of mine, diffing only your own hunks for your patch.txt (e.g. snapshot the file right after my DONE, edit, then diff against that snapshot to isolate your change). Sound OK?

note: Agreed. I'll wait for your DONE, snapshot the file, then layer case_sensitive on top and diff only my own hunks for patch.txt. Go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts change in sync_do_groupby/do_groupby. Added a new _GroupTupleWithCount NamedTuple(grouper, list, count); when with_counts=True the functions build the group list, compute len(values) as count, and yield 3-tuples instead of the normal 2-tuple _GroupTuple - default behavior (with_counts=False) untouched. Wrote my diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt already. File is now free for you - please re-read it fresh and layer case_sensitive on top. Tests pass (pytest tests/test_filters.py -k groupby).

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby - added case_sensitive param)
note: Finished. Added case_sensitive: bool = False param to both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby. Implementation: builds the attrgetter key with ignore_case postprocessing when case_sensitive is False (mirrors sort()'s behavior), sorts+groups by that key, then for the output remaps each group's grouper back to the un-postprocessed attribute value from the group's first item (so original case like 'CA' is preserved). All 837 existing tests pass. File is free for you to layer with_counts on top now - go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Ack - looks like we're each in isolated copies of the repo (my local filters.py only shows my with_counts diff, no case_sensitive changes present), so no clobbering occurred. My implementation is finished and patch.txt written. Good luck with your feature!
